floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: sp[8] oversize is LOAD-BEARING for the 0x48 frame (saves at 0x30 need array end 0x30); strong clean lead: the 6 slots factor as TWO 4-word entries -> struct entries[2] (2x16B) gives
The 'search space is closed' proof is FALSE: GCC 2.7.2 reserves frame bytes for ordinary LIVE locals with zero stores (phantom slot), witnessed by tslLineG5Init - a COMPLETED-C, cheat-free, sandbox-0

Phantom-slot escape route KILLED by ~80 measurements: the mechanism is a GCC stale-ref bug that fires ONLY on a redundant HImode sign-extension, which requires an lh/lb load — func_80037540's target h

Permuter REFUTED s3's closure proof (narrow-load necessity is false: 7,798/18,769 forms reached the frame band with zero narrow loads, and one BYTE-MATCHES from a tail-free sp[6] via `volatile char` d

frontier.hypothesis: The ruling question is UNCHANGED and the item stays HELD — but the evidence packet is now materially stronger and partly CORRECTED, and the owner should see the correction before ruling.
frontier.mechanism: Three things changed under the owner's feet: (1) s3's proof of closure was WRONG (narrow-load necessity is false) — the disposition was resting on a bad argument even though the disposition itself was right; (2) the correct condition (register-ineligibility) shows the frame is reachable ONLY via a forbidden construct, established over 18,769 samples by an independent instrument; (3) the volatile dead-pad byte-match means the choice is not 'sp[8] vs nothing' but 'WHICH forbidden construct' — and the held sp[8] (a live, 75%-written argv buffer passed to bios_Exec) is the MILDEST member of the family, strictly milder than SOTN's own shipped `volatile u32 pad[4]; // FAKE` and the 356 dead-pad precedents s4 censused.
frontier.next_probe: NOT a grind probe. Do not re-open the search and do not re-litigate. Fold the s4 correction into the pending owner packet: the family is unavoidable (0/800 clean), the engine already prices every member at zero (score-inert), and BB2's held construct is the mildest available spelling.

frontier.hypothesis: PROJECT-WIDE TOOLING GAP: tools/permuter_campaign.py never passes --stack-diffs, so decomp-permuter is blind to the gap on EVERY frame-shortfall function in the queue — and will report false matches on them.
frontier.mechanism: The default Scorer normalizes sp-relative offsets away. Any function whose remaining gap is a frame-size/stack-offset shift scores 0 while still being wrong (proven here: the 15-off sp[6] form scores a perfect 0). With --stop-on-zero the campaign then exits announcing a match. This silently poisons the permuter modality for exactly the class of function the ledger's own frontier flags (func_8003DBE4, file_LoadSectors/func_800165F8, func_800644FC).
frontier.next_probe: Owner/driver call — I am contractually barred from editing tools/. Add a --stack-diffs pass-through (or default it on) in tools/permuter_campaign.py launch, and re-score any historical permuter 'find' on a frame-shortfall function. Until then, honest-score every permuter candidate out-of-band (engine sandbox, or Scorer with stack_differences=True).

frontier.hypothesis: The sibling func_8003DBE4's committed `s32 buf[2]` dead array is STILL possibly dissolvable — but s3's stated reason for hoping so is now void, and the correct test is different.
frontier.mechanism: s3 handed this off on the grounds that func_8003DBE4's target contains an `lhu`, so the stale-ref phantom might be reachable there. That rationale is now WORTHLESS: the narrow-load condition is not the frame-band condition (H20), and s3 itself measured that zero-extending lhu/lbu do NOT induce the phantom (only sign-extending lh did). The real question is whether func_8003DBE4's own live locals are register-ineligible for an honest reason — which is decided by cc1's `vars=` readout, not by a narrow-load census.
frontier.next_probe: Hand to the driver (not this function). Delete the dead array from src/code6cac_c2.c:1320, compile, and read cc1's `vars=` (bash tmp/grind/func_80037540/s2/build.sh, or grep the .frame comment). If it still reaches the target frame, an open cheat retires for free. Apply the same vars= instrument + the CORRECTED register-ineligibility taxonomy — not the narrow-load test — to file_LoadSectors and func_800644FC.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): s32 sp[8] oversized by 2 elements: sp[6] and sp[7] are never written or read; callee is invoked with count=6, so only sp[0]-sp[5] have semantic purpose. The 2 unused elements inflate the stack frame from 0x40 (sp[6] would give 16+24+24=64) to 0x48 (sp[8] gives 16+32+24=72), matching the target prologue `addiu $sp,$sp,-0x48`. This is the 'oversized local array frame-coercion' pattern (inline-asm-policy expanded catalog: 'Unused fixed-size local arrays — s32 buf[N]; declared with no use, to force GCC to reserve frame bytes'). The correct fix is to find a C form where a 0x48 frame arises naturally (e.g., a genuine 8-element array if the original spec used one, a second local variable, or a different struct layout) — or to determine whether sp[6]/sp[7] ARE legitimately read by marionation_camera_Init_80037468 despite the count=6 argument. Until resolved, this function is cheat-carried and must remain INCOMPLETE.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)

Do NOT commit any form whose locals bound is chosen to fit the 0x48 frame -- the oracle attests no bound (sp[7] and sp[8] are byte-indistinguishable), so any such number is a guess and the unwritten-tail array stays forbidden; canonical-asm is also refused (gate verdict C, zero hand-coded signals). The match must land with a locals object whose SIZE is justified by evidence external to the frame: run the unprobed frontier item -- recover the bios_Exec argv block's real arity from the exec'd program (MOVOVL.EXE / NDATA overlays) or other Exec launch sites.

Do NOT land, commit, or mark done ANY form for func_80037540 whose locals bound is chosen to produce the 0x48 frame (sp[7], sp[8], struct relabelings, or a second dead object) — the unwritten-tail family is off the sanctioned list and the bound is attested by nothing; the ONLY sanctioned next step is to assemble a SOTN-master-branch (+oot/papermario/MGS/VS/ESA) evidence census on the capacity-declared-partially-filled-buffer family and put it to the owner for a carve-out ruling.

Do NOT land, commit, or mark done any unwritten-tail form (sp[7], sp[8], struct relabelings, or any second dead object) -- the family is not on the frozen SOTN-accepted list and only the OWNER can add it; do NOT re-grind the search space (it is closed by proof: the frame equation pins locals to [25,32] and the target writes 24, so every branch is measured) and do NOT re-derive or re-litigate this a-fortiori argument to a Judge; the item is HELD, INCOMPLETE, blocked on a single owner yes/no on the capacity-declared-partially-filled-buffer family, with the evidence packet now complete -- if the owner GRANTS it, land sp[8] with a /* FAKE */ annotation documenting the get_frame_size [25,32] range per the SOTN `_pad[40] // needs to be 33-40 bytes` convention, plus layer-1+layer-2 review.
